description:
state_complex2 shows a major layout problem: Selkie renders all states in a single vertical column, producing a diagram 1286px wide vs the reference's 716px (80% wider, aspect ratio 0.67 vs 0.42).

The reference lays out sibling states (e.g., Failed/Paused/Completed, Resume/Cancel) horizontally when they're at the same nesting level. Selkie instead stacks everything vertically.

This is the highest-impact state diagram issue — it makes complex state diagrams nearly unrecognizable.

Key files:
- src/render/state.rs (layout logic)
- Reference: reference-implementations/mermaid/packages/mermaid/src/diagrams/state/stateRenderer-v3-unified.ts

Specific composite issues from eval:
- Composite 'Idle' size: ref 700x1461, selkie 1186x1674 (width 70% diff)
- Composite 'Processing' size: ref 600x1233, selkie 830x1425 (width 38% diff)
- Nested composite 'Processing' not centered in 'Idle': -137px offset (ref: 30px)
title: State: fix layout of sibling states within composite groups (complex2 width 80% off)
